name: C. Wiesler's Tavern

hours.note: Est. 1846. Friday fish fry — grilled or beer-battered. Your Place in History.

selloPersonality: hand-stamped tavern seal, worn oak bar top, Germanic crest

stamp.welcomeLine: Wisconsin wasn't a state yet. Wiesler's was already open.
stamp.subLine: The oldest stop on the avenue. The fish fry has been here longer than you think.

story.headline: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER — The southern anchor. Washington Avenue starts here.
story.body: C. Wiesler's has been on this corner since 1846 — longer than Wisconsin has been a state. The antique bar, cash register, and old general store tables are original. The outdoor deck is Cedarburg's first. The Friday fish fry is a weekly institution. This is where the walk begins.
story.insiderTip: The Friday fish fry: grilled or beer-battered haddock, tilapia, or walleye. Come before 6pm or wait for a table. The booth in the back left corner has the best light.
story.parkingNote: Own parking lot — rare for Washington Avenue. Note: Washington Ave south of downtown under road construction through Summer 2026. Use McKinley Blvd from Pioneer Rd as alternate approach.

name: Cedarburg Cultural Center

hours.note: Free gallery admission. First Fridays: live music, free for members, $5 non-members.

selloPersonality: Arts & Music & History seal, ink and parchment, community gathering mark

stamp.subLine: Art, music, and history in the heart of downtown. Free to walk in.

story.headline: Forty years as Cedarburg's living room.
story.body: The Cedarburg Cultural Center has been the community anchor for 40 years — rotating art exhibits, live Main Stage performances, First Fridays concerts, open mic nights, and classes for kids and adults. The gallery is free and always changing. The nonprofit mission is genuine: this place exists to celebrate community, arts, and local heritage.
story.insiderTip: Check the event calendar before you visit — there's almost always something happening. First Fridays bring a local band and a room full of people who know each other. Walk in like you belong.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Washington Ave. Free and plentiful outside peak festival weekends.

name: Union House

hours.note: Thu–Sun only. Dinner and bar. Unlimited tapas available ($40/person, 2hr limit).

selloPersonality: modern tapas seal, shareable plates, city energy in a small town

stamp.subLine: Craft cocktails, shareable plates. Feels like somewhere bigger.

story.headline: Modern tapas in a preserved historic building.
story.body: Union House brought something unexpected to Cedarburg — a proper craft cocktail and shareable-plate destination that feels like it could be in Milwaukee or Chicago, but chose a 19th-century storefront on Washington Avenue instead. The menu changes seasonally. The cocktails are award-winning. Managing partner Chris Homayouni built it for people who want city energy in a small-town room.
story.insiderTip: The unlimited tapas is the move for a group — $40/person, two hours, choose from cheese, meat, bread, soups, and plates. The Sunday brunch charcuterie board is excellent for two. Reservations recommended on weekends.

name: Rivoli Theatre

hours.note: All movies $4.00. Tuesdays $2.50. Run by 900+ local volunteers. Cash and card.

selloPersonality: 1936 Art Deco marquee stamp, single screen, hand-lettered

stamp.welcomeLine: 900 volunteers. $4 tickets. The town wouldn't let it close.
stamp.subLine: Marcus left in 2007. Cedarburg bought it back. Tuesday nights sell out.

story.headline: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER — 900 volunteers. $4 movies since 1936.
story.body: The Rivoli opened January 11, 1936, in a late-1880s building originally a Cedarburg general store. Marcus Theatres closed it in 2007. The community bought it back — $1,000,000 raised by locals, professionals donating services, neighbors chipping in. Today 900+ volunteers from Cedarburg and surrounding areas run every ticket window and concession stand. The professional projectionist is the only paid role. Tickets are $4. Tuesdays $2.50. The town will not let this place close.
story.insiderTip: Tuesday nights at $2.50 — sell out regularly for popular films. Get there by 6:30. The popcorn is $2 and the candy is $1. Sit in the center section, third row from the back. The volunteers are genuinely happy to see you.
story.parkingNote: Free parking throughout downtown Cedarburg. Walk from anywhere on Washington Ave.

name: Handen Distillery

hours.note: Women-owned. 30+ awards. Inside a 1920s Ford garage. Flight or cocktail.

selloPersonality: hand-crafted spirit label, Germanic precision, copper still silhouette

stamp.subLine: Women-owned. Potato vodka. Barrel-aged gin. The Spirit of Cedarburg.

story.headline: Thirty-plus awards. A 1920s Ford garage. Two women who built this from scratch.
story.body: Lisa Tank and Mary-Kay Bourbulas met at an investment firm in Chicago and built Handen together — vodka distilled from potatoes for a luscious, floral mouthfeel; Original Gin that converts skeptics with citrus and botanicals; single-barrel whiskeys that the head distiller selects personally. The tasting room is inside a beautifully preserved 1920s Ford dealership. The name means hands in German — a nod to Cedarburg's Germanic roots and to the people who built everything here by hand.
story.insiderTip: Order the flight before you commit to a bottle. The Mill Race Whiskey and Original Gin are the most-loved by regulars. Ask about the single-barrel picks — they don't last long. To-go cocktail kits available: Wisconsin Old Fashioned, Best in Show G&T.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Washington Ave. Suite B entrance.

name: Cedarburg Coffee Roastery

hours.note: Roasting on-site since 1999. Cedarburg Blend and Jamaican Blue Mountain. Watch the roaster.

selloPersonality: antique coffee roaster stamp, old-world parchment, beans in circle

stamp.subLine: Roasting on Washington Avenue since 1999. Watch the beans spin.

story.headline: The roaster is in the room. The beans are always fresh.
story.body: Cedarburg Coffee Roastery has been roasting on-site since 1999 — arabica beans from Guatemala, Costa Rica, Ethiopia, Kenya, and exotics like Jamaican Blue Mountain and Hawaiian Kona. The roaster sits center-stage behind the bar, surrounded by barstools. Artwork covers the walls. The Cedarburg Blend is their signature: light and dark together, flavorful and balanced. Buy a bag to go — ground or whole bean.
story.insiderTip: The Cedarburg Blend is the one to take home. The iced caramel macchiato gets consistent praise. Small space — come before the weekend festival crowds or you'll be waiting for a seat.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Washington Ave. Metered on weekends during festivals.

name: Fiddleheads Coffee

hours.note: Family-owned since 1996. Cream city brick patio. Community room upstairs available.

selloPersonality: fiddlehead fern unfurling, botanical illustration, Wisconsin woodland

stamp.subLine: On this corner since 1996. Cream city brick and antique art.

story.headline: Family-owned since 1996. Antique art, a cream city brick patio, and the best patio seat on the avenue.
story.body: Fiddleheads started in Thiensville in 1996 and grew into a specialty coffee company with its own small-batch roastery and artisan bakery. The Cedarburg location sits in an old house on Washington Avenue — cream city brick exterior, antique art inside, a second-floor community room, and a patio for warm months. The chai is clove-heavy and exactly right. The peanut butter cookies have a following.
story.insiderTip: The patio in good weather is the best seat on Washington Ave — sit facing the avenue and watch the street. The chai is spiced with clove and not sweet by default. The second-floor room can be reserved for small events.
story.parkingNote: Street parking. Note they're next door to the Cedarburg Coffee Roastery — two coffee stops at almost the same address.

name: The Stilt House

hours.note: Patio over Cedar Creek. 150+ craft beers. Fish fry Fridays. Stilt Burger is the order.

selloPersonality: building-on-stilts mark, Cedar Creek below, gastropub warmth

stamp.subLine: Built on stilts over Cedar Creek. 150 beers. The Stilt Burger.

story.headline: Why is it on stilts? Nick Schuh asked for a second floor. The city said no. He raised the building instead.
story.body: In the early 1900s, Nick Schuh wanted to expand his tavern. The city denied the second-floor addition. He put the whole building on stilts and built a first floor underneath. The Stilt House has been there ever since — now a gastropub with 150+ American craft beers, a seasonal menu driven by local ingredients, craft cocktails, and a Cedar Creek patio that's the best outdoor seat north of the covered bridge. Gordon Goggin and Tricia Dooley have run it since 2013.
story.insiderTip: The Stilt Burger is the thing to order. The Friday fish fry — Potato Chip Crusted Cod is the sleeper pick. The creek patio in warm weather: come at 11am before it fills. The bread pudding has been called 'off the chart' by regulars.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Washington Ave. The creek-side patio is accessible from the back.

name: Cedarburg Art Museum

hours.note: Free admission. 1898 Wittenberg-Jochem Mansion. Wisconsin art and history.

selloPersonality: turret mansion mark, Romanesque-Queen Anne, Wisconsin art heritage

stamp.subLine: Free admission. An 1898 mansion full of Wisconsin art.

story.headline: Wisconsin art in an 1898 mansion with a turret, stained glass, and hand-painted ceilings.
story.body: The Cedarburg Art Museum lives inside the Wittenberg-Jochem Mansion — Romanesque, Queen Anne, and Classical architecture combined, built in 1898. The original oak woodwork survived decades as apartments. The hand-painted canvases on the foyer ceiling survived. The stained glass on the open staircase survived. Today it holds Wisconsin art: rotating exhibitions, a juried annual show, educational programs, and a museum gift shop with work by local artists. Admission is free.
story.insiderTip: The second and third floor windows give a bird's-eye view of Cedarburg's historic district. The Summer Beer Garden brings live music to the grounds in warm months. Check the exhibition calendar — the annual juried show is a strong survey of Wisconsin work.
story.parkingNote: Street parking on Washington Ave. Just north of the covered bridge area.

name: Cedar Creek Winery

hours.note: Tastings and tours daily. Inside the 1864 Cedar Creek Settlement woolen mill. Pet-friendly outdoors.

selloPersonality: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER — John to supply anchor copy

stamp.welcomeLine: The wool is gone. The limestone cellars stayed.
stamp.subLine: 1864. The mill that became a winery. This is where the walk ends.

story.headline: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER — The mill. 1864. The northern terminus.
story.body: Cedar Creek Winery sits inside the Cedar Creek Settlement — a 150-year-old woolen mill listed on the National Register of Historic Places. The limestone cellars age the wine. Daily tastings take you from the tasting room down into those cellars. The Cedarburg Spice and Strawberry Blush are the seasonal signatures. Pet-friendly at the outdoor tables. One of America's most noted regional wineries — that's not marketing copy, it's the honest reputation built over decades.
story.insiderTip: The tour goes into the limestone cellars — worth doing even if you've tasted before. The Cedarburg Spice is the bottle to take home. The Cedar Creek Settlement has 20+ shops around the winery — budget an extra hour for the mill.
story.parkingNote: Cedar Creek Settlement lot on Bridge Rd. Short walk from Washington Ave via the covered bridge.
